title: paeonia mascula subsp. kayae
intro: Paeonia mascula subsp. kayae, also known as Turkish peony, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the genus Paeonia, characterized by its red or reddish-purple flowers and thick, hairy leaves. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving pain and menstrual cramps, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. Paeonia mascula subsp. kayae can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or salve, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
